steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left side: independent clause — “travelers flew out of major urban airports with increasing frequency”
Right side: independent clause — “San Diego International Airport in San Diego, California, saw a 49.7 percent increase...”

steps.title: Apply the transition rule
steps.content:
A transition word like “For example” requires a period before it (when beginning a new sentence) and a comma after it.

Choice C correctly uses “frequency. For example,” — this establishes the example as a supporting illustration of the preceding claim.

Choices A and B incorrectly place “for example” at the end of the first clause, while choice D creates a comma splice by joining the ideas with only commas.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
